sources.title: EUR-Lex: EU Artificial Intelligence Act Full Text
European Commission: AI Regulatory Framework Overview
Holistic AI: EU AI Act Compliance Guide
IAPP: EU AI Act Practical Analysis

result.summary: The EU AI Act establishes a risk-based regulatory framework with four tiers (unacceptable, high-risk, limited, minimal), requiring conformity assessments, transparency obligations, and human oversight mechanisms, with phased enforcement beginning February 2025.
key_findings: High-risk AI systems (hiring, credit scoring, law enforcement) require mandatory conformity assessments, data governance documentation, and ongoing monitoring with potential fines up to 35M EUR or 7% of global turnover

General-purpose AI models (including LLMs) face transparency requirements including technical documentation, copyright compliance summaries, and systemic risk assessments for models trained above 10^25 FLOPs
Enforcement timeline is phased: prohibited practices banned by February 2025, GPAI obligations by August 2025, and full high-risk system requirements by August 2026
